mission: Freeze the authoritative external-integration assumption ledger and degraded-mode default pack so Vecells can execute mock-now simulator work and later live-provider work without silent truth drift.

assumptions.summary: Authoritative proof, insufficient evidence, ambiguity, degraded continuity, and manual fallback remain distinct states and cannot be collapsed into generic success or generic failure.
Simulator rows and future live-provider rows share one contract ledger; live onboarding may only override adapter-facing details through explicit conflict registration.
Patient, staff, and support surfaces may differ in calmness and detail, but none may imply finality when the governing dependency row remains ambiguous, degraded, or blocked.

fallback_ladder.meaning: Current authoritative proof objects are present and the dependency can support truthful calmness.
fallback_ladder.ui_posture: Only this rung may support final calm completion.

fallback_ladder.meaning: Signals exist but do not satisfy the authoritative proof contract, or they contradict the current tuple.
fallback_ladder.ui_posture: Pending, review, or held posture stays explicit.

fallback_ladder.meaning: The system can continue in a bounded downgraded mode without pretending the external truth has settled.
fallback_ladder.ui_posture: Read-only, degraded, or continuity-safe posture remains explicit.

fallback_ladder.meaning: A named human-owned path, assisted action, or operational handoff becomes the governing route.
fallback_ladder.ui_posture: Manual assistance and evidence capture become visible, not hidden in notes.

identity_access.label: Identity & access
identity_access.description: Authentication and governed enrichment defaults that decide whether writable authority may exist at all.

communications.label: Communications
communications.description: Patient and staff delivery rails where send acceptance is weaker than delivery truth.

evidence_processing.label: Evidence processing
evidence_processing.description: Transcript and artifact scanning seams where readiness, quarantine, and replay remain explicit.

gp_booking.label: GP booking
gp_booking.description: IM1, supplier, and capacity assumptions that gate booking confirmation truth.

messaging_transport.label: Messaging transport
messaging_transport.description: Cross-organisation messaging and acknowledgement rails where transport evidence never settles business truth alone.

pharmacy.label: Pharmacy
pharmacy.description: Choice, dispatch, outcome, and urgent-return defaults with explicit continuity-safe fallback.

embedded_channels.label: Embedded channels
embedded_channels.description: Deferred embedded-channel assumptions kept explicit without widening current scope.

assurance_watch.label: Assurance watch
assurance_watch.description: Watchlist and optional-vendor dependencies that must fail closed to observe-only, shadow-only, or release-blocking posture.

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Authenticate patients and step up authority before writable portal or recovery actions continue.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current SessionEstablishmentDecision plus current Session, IdentityBinding, and RouteIntentBinding showing writable authority for the same subject and lineage.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Subject claim exists without settled writable authority
ambiguity_modes: ConsentNotGiven or access_denied returns
Subject mismatch or stale subject binding version
claim_pending or auth_read_only posture after successful auth
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Freeze the route in claim-pending or auth-read-only posture until writable authority is re-proven for the same subject and route intent.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Route to support-assisted recovery or a secure-link continuation path without widening identity truth.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: Show sign-in succeeded but access is still being confirmed; do not imply full portal ownership yet.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Display the current binding state, route-intent fence, and any claim-pending blocker before staff attempt recovery.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Surface the redirect review, partner approval state, and subject-lineage mismatch cues needed for assisted recovery.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: NHS login authentication rail is closure-blocking: no calm patient close, booking confirmation, dispatch settlement, or route completion may proceed while proof remains ambiguous, degraded, or manual-only.
simulator_counterparts: NHS login auth and session twin (proof_twin, hybrid_contract_twin)
live_upgrade_evidence: Partner approval and current redirect inventory
Technical conformance evidence
Environment-specific callback and session-parity rehearsal
Fresh gate snapshot stamped with the capture date
Official label checks still match the current NHS login guidance
Named redirect review reference linked to the active route family
Scope approval reference tied to the same environment target
non_negotiable_assumptions: Callback success alone never equals writable authority
Claim-pending and auth-read-only remain separate post-auth states
Route-intent binding remains mandatory for write access
provider_variability_envelope: Official branding or consent-page chrome outside Vecells control
live_gate_or_approval_required: Required env: nhs login named approver, nhs login environment target, allow real provider mutation
Blocked gates: LIVE_GATE_EXTERNAL_FOUNDATION_WITHHELD, LIVE_GATE_NHS_LOGIN_PARTNER_APPROVED, LIVE_GATE_ENVIRONMENT_TARGET_MISSING, LIVE_GATE_MUTATION_FLAG_DISABLED, LIVE_GATE_IM1_SCAL_APPROVED
Review gates: LIVE_GATE_REDIRECT_URI_REVIEW, LIVE_GATE_TECHNICAL_CONFORMANCE_PENDING
dependencies.explicit_override_rule: Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Client identifiers and signing material, Environment-scoped redirect URIs, Official branding or consent-page chrome outside Vecells control; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout.
manual_checkpoints: Partner approval before non-mock credentials are issued
Redirect-URI and scope review against live environment inventory
non_authoritative_signals: Subject claim exists without settled writable authority
fallback_or_recovery_modes: bounded_recovery with explicit post-auth next step
auth_read_only or claim_pending shell posture
secure-link or support-assisted recovery without pretending writable ownership
dependencies.notes: NHS login remains an authentication rail only; it does not replace patient linkage, PDS, or claim ownership.

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Optionally enrich patient-linking evidence with governed PDS demographics after legal basis and onboarding are complete.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: A legally permitted PDS lookup correlated to the current linkage candidate set and accepted as supporting evidence by IdentityBindingAuthority.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Single local demographic candidate without sufficient confidence
ambiguity_modes: Legal basis or onboarding still incomplete
PDS response conflicts with local or telephony evidence
Feature flag disabled for the current tenant or route
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Keep enrichment fully off and rely on local matching only; do not widen patient identity confidence from incomplete or legally unapproved PDS signals.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Support-assisted correction or governed local review without changing the baseline identity contract.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: Continue with the baseline identity journey and avoid mentioning unseen PDS enrichment.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Mark enrichment withheld, feature-flagged, or review-required and preserve local matching evidence separately.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Expose legal-basis, access-mode, and wrong-patient rollback readiness requirements before enabling enrichment.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: Optional PDS enrichment seam stays feature-flagged or deferred; if enabled later, ambiguity still blocks truthful close and must not be flattened into optional success.

Named approver and environment
Wrong-patient and rollback readiness evidence
Draft receipt linked to the chosen access mode
Current hazard and risk log references attached to the same draft
Named legal or governance approval reference
Rollback rehearsal evidence tied to the same route-bound access mode
non_negotiable_assumptions: PDS stays optional and feature-flagged
Local binding and NHS login remain the baseline identity authority
Wrong-patient repair holds remain explicit even when enrichment is enabled
live_gate_or_approval_required: Required env: pds named approver, pds environment target, pds organisation ods, pds use case owner, allow real provider mutation

Blocked gates: GATE_EXTERNAL_TO_FOUNDATION, LIVE_GATE_PDS_LEGAL_BASIS_APPROVED, PDS_LIVE_GATE_NAMED_APPROVER_PRESENT, PDS_LIVE_GATE_ENVIRONMENT_TARGET_PRESENT, PDS_LIVE_GATE_ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION

Review gates: PDS_LIVE_GATE_ACCESS_MODE_SELECTED, PDS_LIVE_GATE_HAZARD_LOG_CURRENT, PDS_LIVE_GATE_RISK_LOGS_CURRENT, PDS_LIVE_GATE_WRONG_PATIENT_PLAN_CURRENT, PDS_LIVE_GATE_SECURE_NETWORK_PATH_PLANNED, PDS_LIVE_GATE_ROLLBACK_REHEARSED
dependencies.explicit_override_rule: Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Access mode and network path, Certificate or credential profile, Demographic field mapping breadth; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout.
manual_checkpoints: Legal basis and governance approval before sandbox or live access
Tenant feature-flag review before any patient-visible enrichment
non_authoritative_signals: Single local demographic candidate without sufficient confidence
fallback_or_recovery_modes: Local matching only with partial_match or review posture
Support-assisted correction without widening identity truth
Keep enrichment fully off while preserving baseline identity flow
dependencies.notes: Optional by corpus law: PDS is an enrichment seam, not a baseline blocker and not a substitute for NHS login or local binding governance.

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Capture phone-origin demand, callback attempts, IVR routing, voicemail, and recording availability inside the same governed request lineage.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current CallSession evidence or CallbackOutcomeEvidenceBundle linked to AdapterReceiptCheckpoint and the current lineage-bound callback or intake objects.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Recording promised but not yet fetched or verified
ambiguity_modes: recording_missing or transcript_degraded
Duplicate or contradictory callback outcomes
urgent_live_only posture without routine evidence readiness
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Hold the request in callback, audio-review, or urgent-live-only posture when recording availability is weaker than evidence readiness.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Escalate to governed manual callback or urgent live diversion with explicit operator ownership.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: Acknowledge that contact is in progress or needs follow-up; do not imply clinically usable evidence exists yet.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Show callback lineage, recording availability, and urgent preemption state separately from contact success.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Expose vendor, number, spend, and webhook readiness blockers before any live telephony mutation continues.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: Telephony, IVR, and call-recording provider is closure-blocking: no calm patient close, booking confirmation, dispatch settlement, or route completion may proceed while proof remains ambiguous, degraded, or manual-only.

Webhook security and replay posture

Urgent-live preemption outranks routine continuation
Recording availability stays weaker than evidence readiness

Webhook signatures and callback payload shape

dependencies.explicit_override_rule: Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Number inventory and vendor account identifiers, Webhook signatures and callback payload shape, Recording retrieval mechanics; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout.

Secure-link continuation where route authority allows

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Produce transcript readiness and derived facts from recordings while keeping original audio authoritative.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: TelephonyTranscriptReadinessRecord with clinically sufficient coverage accepted by TelephonyEvidenceReadinessAssessment(usabilityState = safety_usable).
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Model returned text without a matching readiness assessment
ambiguity_modes: Coverage is partial or clinically insufficient
Contradictory transcript reruns or stale derivation packages
Manual review still required before routine promotion
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Keep transcript output in degraded, manual-review, or continuation-challenge posture until readiness is explicitly safety-usable.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Route to manual transcription or audio review and keep superseded transcript runs visible for comparison.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: No patient-facing completion state may depend on transcript availability alone.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Flag transcript coverage, supersession, and review requirements before any derived facts are treated as dependable.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Expose region, retention, callback security, and operator-acknowledgement requirements for live provider rollout.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: Transcript and derived-facts processing provider is closure-blocking: no calm patient close, booking confirmation, dispatch settlement, or route completion may proceed while proof remains ambiguous, degraded, or manual-only.

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Deliver seeded telephony continuation links and selected patient prompts through a controlled outbound SMS rail.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current AdapterReceiptCheckpoint plus the governing AccessGrant or CommunicationEnvelope still current for the same route and visibility tuple.
ambiguity_modes: Wrong-recipient or stale-number risk
Bounced, expired, or disputed delivery
Link redemption fenced by subject or session drift
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Withdraw seeded continuation trust and fall back to challenge-based continuation or another governed contact route when recipient or delivery truth is disputed.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Support reissue or bounded callback under the same grant lineage rather than silent re-send.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: Explain that the link or message may need to be reissued; do not imply the message safely reached the intended recipient.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Keep wrong-recipient suspicion, delivery ambiguity, and redemption fences explicit before reissuing.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Show sender ownership, spend control, and repair-policy blockers required for live SMS widening.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: SMS delivery provider stays feature-flagged or deferred; if enabled later, ambiguity still blocks truthful close and must not be flattened into optional success.

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Scan uploads and recordings before they leave quarantine or become available to downstream review and promotion.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current artifact quarantine outcome showing the capture bundle or recording is safe, readable enough, or explicitly quarantined with governed next action.
ambiguity_modes: Scan timed out or returned unsupported
File is quarantined or unreadable
Conflicting scan evidence across retries
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Fail closed into quarantined, unreadable, or reacquire-required posture until the current artifact verdict is explicit.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Manual review, re-upload, or release-from-quarantine under named operator approval only.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: No patient-facing progress claim may rely on an unverified artifact becoming usable.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Display quarantine, timeout, and conflicting verdict states distinctly from ordinary processing delays.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Expose storage-scope, quarantine-policy, and mutation-gate blockers before widening live scanning.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: Malware and artifact scanning provider blocks onboarding, release, or downstream operator truth; patient-visible calmness may continue only when no new external truth is implied.

dependencies.dependency_name: IM1 Pairing programme and prerequisite path
dependencies.canonical_purpose: Secure the prerequisites, SCAL, mock API, supported test, assurance, and rollout gates required before GP supplier integrations become live.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current pairing evidence pack showing the exact supplier, organisation, and environment have cleared the required IM1 stage for the targeted capability set.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Mock documentation received without supported-test or assurance completion
ambiguity_modes: SCAL or model-interface prerequisites still pending
Supported-test or assurance incomplete for the selected supplier path
Live rollout not yet approved for the supplier or organisation pair
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Keep the path simulator-only, supported-test-only, or assisted-only until the exact supplier and environment clear the IM1 stage pack.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Hub fallback or human-assisted booking handling where policy allows, with no false live-availability claim.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: Do not promise direct supplier booking while programme prerequisites remain incomplete.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Expose the current supplier, stage, and supported-test status before surfacing live-path availability.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Show sponsor, commercial owner, and approver gaps that still block live supplier movement.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: IM1 Pairing programme and prerequisite path is closure-blocking: no calm patient close, booking confirmation, dispatch settlement, or route completion may proceed while proof remains ambiguous, degraded, or manual-only.

non_negotiable_assumptions: Supplier capability matrices remain explicit
Hold expiry and commit ambiguity stay separate from calm confirmation
Local waitlist and fallback law remain core-owned

Search or hold feature flags by supplier estate

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Provide the dedicated monitored email, professional number, or equivalent local route for urgent and unsupported pharmacy returns.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current urgent-return or bounce-back evidence acknowledged on the configured route and bound to the active PharmacyCase or reopened request lineage.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Email sent to the safety-net mailbox
ambiguity_modes: Urgent return not yet acknowledged on the current route
Stale or misconfigured monitored email or professional number
Contact-route repair blocks the expected return channel
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Treat the route as unavailable or unsafe until urgent-return ownership and current acknowledgement are explicit.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Immediate supervisor or duty escalation by phone when the monitored digital route is unavailable or unacknowledged.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: Acknowledge that urgent clinical follow-up is being escalated; do not imply a mailbox alone settled the return.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Keep route ownership, acknowledgement, and rehearseability visible before relying on the safety-net route.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Expose monitored address or number ownership, rehearsal evidence, and escalation expectations for urgent return.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: Pharmacy urgent-return and professional-contact routes is closure-blocking: no calm patient close, booking confirmation, dispatch settlement, or route completion may proceed while proof remains ambiguous, degraded, or manual-only.
simulator_counterparts: No current simulator counterpart; explicit watch-only contract pack only
live_upgrade_evidence: Fresh route snapshot showing the current preferred, fallback, and manual paths
Official label checks still match current pharmacy guidance
Named transport route tuple or provider receipt
Current dispatch proof thresholds and approver evidence bound to the same attempt
non_negotiable_assumptions: Urgent return remains a human-operated safety net and cannot collapse into ordinary Update Record semantics
Acknowledgement on the current route is required before the case can look settled
Phone escalation remains explicit when the monitored digital path is unavailable
provider_variability_envelope: Practice-level rota and escalation ownership details
Route acknowledgement wording and local handoff cadence

dependencies.canonical_purpose: Expose the same patient journeys inside NHS App through embedded shell, SSO handoff, site links, and governed bridge capability negotiation.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current NHSAppIntegrationManifest, PatientEmbeddedNavEligibility, and PatientEmbeddedSessionProjection under the approved manifest, release, and bridge-capability tuple.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: User-agent or query hint suggests embedded mode
Manifest file exists without current eligibility and continuity proof
ambiguity_modes: Manifest drift across Sandpit, AOS, and live

dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Drop to safe browser handoff, read-only embed, or placeholder-only posture when manifest, bridge, or embedded continuity evidence drifts.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Use governed release review and manual site-link confirmation before widening embedded routes.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: Explain that the service may continue in the browser if the embedded route is not currently eligible.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Expose manifest stage, embedded eligibility, and bridge-capability mismatches without masking them as generic channel errors.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Show commissioning, accessibility, demo-environment, and NHS login readiness blockers for the current embedded stage.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: NHS App embedded-channel ecosystem stays feature-flagged or deferred; if enabled later, ambiguity still blocks truthful close and must not be flattened into optional success.

dependencies.dependency_name: Assistive model vendor and subprocessor family
dependencies.canonical_purpose: Optionally provide bounded assistive inference and drafting services without making core workflow correctness depend on model vendors.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current AssistiveCapabilityTrustEnvelope plus AssistiveCapabilityRolloutVerdict and supplier-assurance freshness for the exact route, cohort, and watch tuple.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Watch tuple or rollout rung appears green without current trust envelope
ambiguity_modes: trustState = degraded, quarantined, shadow_only, or frozen
Supplier assurance drifted, suspended, or stale
Route-family or cohort rollout evidence is missing or blocked
dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Force assistive capability back to shadow-only, observe-only, provenance-only, or hidden posture whenever the trust envelope is degraded, stale, or quarantined.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Continue the core workflow with no model dependency and preserve provenance-only evidence for later review.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: No patient-facing promise may depend on assistive vendor output being available or trusted.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Show assistive capability as optional, shadowed, or frozen and keep human-authored truth separate from model suggestions.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Expose assurance drift, rollout rung, and kill-switch state before any live cohort widens.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: Assistive model vendor and subprocessor family remains a watch-only or optional contract row; baseline flow continues, but release or rollout widening must stop if its evidence drifts.
simulator_counterparts: No current simulator counterpart; explicit watch-only contract pack only
live_upgrade_evidence: Current supplier assurance pack for the exact route family and cohort

Named approver, live cohort definition, and trust-envelope freshness
non_negotiable_assumptions: Assistive vendors remain optional and may not become a baseline correctness dependency
Shadow-only, observe-only, hidden, or frozen states remain first-class outcomes
Human provenance and rollback paths stay explicit even when a vendor is healthy
provider_variability_envelope: Vendor scoring and inference metadata shape
Drafting or summarisation surface details that remain behind adapter boundaries
live_gate_or_approval_required: Named approver and live cohort definition
Fresh supplier assurance and subprocessor review

dependencies.explicit_override_rule: Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Vendor scoring and inference metadata shape, Subprocessor inventory and region posture, Drafting or summarisation surface details that remain behind adapter boundaries; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout.
manual_checkpoints: Subprocessor and assurance review before any live visible cohort
Rollback and kill-switch proof before widening beyond shadow or observe-only
non_authoritative_signals: Watch tuple or rollout rung appears green without current trust envelope
fallback_or_recovery_modes: observe_only, provenance_only, placeholder_only, or hidden posture
Shadow-only or frozen rollout without widening assistive actionability
Keep core clinical and operational workflow fully complete without model dependency
dependencies.notes: The assistive control plane is in scope, but live model vendors remain optional and must fail to shadow, observe-only, or hidden posture.

dependencies.dependency_name: NHS standards and assurance source set
dependencies.canonical_purpose: Version the external standards, guidance, and supplier-assurance inputs that gate release, onboarding, and evidence admissibility.
dependencies.authoritative_success_proof: Current StandardsVersionMap and StandardsDependencyWatchlist bound to the same assurance-evidence graph and release candidate under review.
insufficient_evidence_patterns: Static checklist copied into local notes

ambiguity_modes: Standards version moved, refreshed, or decommissioned

dependencies.degraded_mode_default: Freeze the affected onboarding, release, or provider assumption pack until the current standards baseline is rechecked and mapped.
dependencies.manual_fallback_default: Open an explicit standards exception or evidence-gap register entry rather than silently proceeding.
dependencies.patient_visible_posture_default: No direct patient copy change; affected release or provider widening stays withheld until standards truth is current.
dependencies.staff_visible_posture_default: Show which assurance or release assumptions are blocked by stale source-of-law evidence.
dependencies.support_visible_posture_default: Expose missing baseline refreshes, suspended supplier assurance, and explicit exception requirements for remediation.
dependencies.closure_blocker_implications: NHS standards and assurance source set blocks onboarding, release, or downstream operator truth; patient-visible calmness may continue only when no new external truth is implied.
